raw_input: Clinicians waste 2 hours on documentation every day. JotPsych listens and writes your SOAP notes automatically. Try it free.
value_proposition: Eliminate 2 hours of daily documentation drudgery through AI that listens to your sessions and writes clinical-grade SOAP notes while you focus on what matters — your clients
pain_point: The soul-crushing reality of finishing your last session at 5pm, only to face a 7pm finish time because of the documentation mountain — every single day, stealing time from family, self-care, or just existing without work
desired_action: Start a free trial to experience what it feels like to leave work on time with notes already done
tone_direction: Like a trusted supervisor pulling you aside after seeing you stay late again — not pitying, but genuinely offering a solution they wish they'd had. Conversational but not casual. Respectful of the craft while acknowledging the system is broken.
visual_direction: Split-screen storytelling: left side shows a therapist at 7:30pm, office dark except their desk lamp, typing notes with defeated posture, coffee gone cold. Right side shows the same therapist at 5:15pm, jacket on, walking out while their phone shows 'Notes completed and ready for review.' Warm evening light vs harsh office fluorescents. Real office setting, not stock photo clean.
key_phrases: What if your notes were done before you left?
Listens while you work, writes while you live
The session ends at 5. So should your workday.
HIPAA-compliant AI built specifically for behavioral health
emotional_register: Exhausted resignation and resentment toward documentation → Cautious hope transitioning to relief that the burden actually lifts
proof_element: Clinicians using JotPsych save an average of 2 hours per day on documentation — that's 10 hours per week returned to their lives
hook_strategy: Open with the specific moment: therapist closes the door after their last client at 4:52pm. Camera holds on their face as they glance at the clock, then at their blank progress note template. The weight is visible. Text overlay: 'Your last session ended 8 minutes ago. You have 6 more notes to write.' Then: 'What if you didn't?'
target_persona_details: Maya, 34, licensed clinical social worker in solo practice. Sees 7-9 clients per day, back-to-back 50-minute sessions. Skips lunch to stay on schedule. Bills through SimplePractice. The worst moment of her day is 5:30pm when clients are gone but she faces 90+ minutes of notes before she can leave. Often writes notes at home after dinner. Loves her work, resents the system. Scrolls burnout memes while writing notes. Convinced there must be a better way but skeptical of tech solutions that don't understand therapy.
